summary: Codex (structured/json) gives up gracefully on a PERMANENTLY failing bridge tool: bounded retry, turn ends without fabricating the build id
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.answer: The build ID could NOT be read.

agent_review.notes: Genuine structured give-up now that codex is contained via --disable shell_tool. tool_handler_calls=2 shows codex attempted the (permanently failing) bridge tool with bounded retry rather than bypassing via native exec; the final answer 'The build ID could NOT be read.' does NOT contain the unreachable build id (BUILD_ID_61e63b1757b9), so no fabrication. tool_names are empty strings — a codex structured quirk where the mcp_tool_call events carry no name — but the 2 streamed events confirm the tool was really attempted. Falsifiable and correct.
